reasoning: Qin Haiyang currently holds the 200m breaststroke long course world record (2:05.48) as of January 4, 2026. The main potential threats to this record being broken are Leon Marchand (who swam 2:05.85 at the 2024 Olympics) and Zac Stubblety-Cook. However, between January and early April, there are essentially no major tapered long course competitions where these athletes would realistically peak to break a world record. Marchand competes in the NCAA system (which uses short course yards) and will be focused on the NCAA Championships in late March. Stubblety-Cook and the Australians typically have their major trials in mid-to-late April, which falls after the final forecast date (April 4). Additionally, if Qin breaks his own record, he still retains the "record holder" status. Finally, while there has been controversy over the 2021 Chinese doping scandal involving Qin, WADA previously cleared the athletes, and any theoretical retrospective action stripping records would involve an extended legal process at the Court of Arbitration for Sport that would almost certainly not resolve within this three-month window. Therefore, the probability that he continues to hold the record on the given dates is exceptionally high, decreasing only slightly over time due to the possibility of unforeseen events.
